pattern: The score of every suffix against the full string is exactly what the Z-function measures, so summing all scores is summing the Z-array.
hints: The score for the suffix of length i is the length of the longest prefix of the whole string that also appears starting at that suffix's position -- that's a match between the string and one of its own suffixes.

There's a linear-time technique for computing, for every starting position, how far it matches the beginning of the string, reusing previously computed matches instead of comparing from scratch each time.

Maintain the rightmost match window found so far; when a new position falls inside it, seed its match length from a previously computed position before extending further by direct comparison, then sum every position's match length, counting the full string's self-match as its own length.

edge_inputs.description: all identical characters maximize every prefix match
no repeated structure at all, only the trivial full match
a single differing character near the end breaks most matches

stress.description: a full-length string over a tiny alphabet, encouraging long partial matches
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
